curation.synopsis: In an outlying manor in the realm of death, a servant — a transplant from the verdant realm of life — watches uneasily as his master returns with a desiccated husk taken from the bough of a hateful Spite-Revenant and installs it as a curiosity in the great hall. From the moment the relic crosses the threshold, the manor's quiet routines begin to fray: footfalls in empty corridors, sap-stains where there should be none, dreams full of grasping roots. The servant, who still remembers the living forests of his birth-realm, recognises the trespass for what it is long before his master will, and the small household closes in on the slow harvest the bough has come to take.

flags.note: Roster marks 'novel' but The Gnarled Bough is a Black Library eShort, later collected in The Resting Places anthology
Roster places this in the W40K domain, but The Gnarled Bough is an Age of Sigmar story set across the realms of Shyish and Ghyran
